title: festuca ovina var. saximontana
intro: Festuca ovina var. saximontana, also known as sheep's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Festuca genus and is native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infection. The health benefits of Festuca ovina var. saximontana include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, this plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds.
